author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: OSSF Scorecard 10/10 program: close every scoring gap from the 2026-07-14 baseline (6.9)

Every non-10 Scorecard check has a child item; agent-executable children shipped; human/community-gated children have their gate documented; aggregate score re-measured after each child closes (weekly cron or workflow_dispatch) and progress noted here; program closes when the score is 10.0 or every remaining gap is formally accepted with rationale on this item

message: Maintainer directive 2026-07-14: gain a 10/10 OpenSSF Scorecard (scorecard.dev/viewer/?uri=github.com/unbraind/pm-cli). Baseline 6.9 measured by the pm-k7dp workflow. Perfect-10 checks already: Dependency-Update-Tool, Maintained, Dangerous-Workflow, Binary-Artifacts, License, Pinned-Dependencies, Vulnerabilities, Packaging, SAST, CI-Tests. Gaps → children: Token-Permissions 0, Code-Review 0, CII-Best-Practices 0, Fuzzing 0, Branch-Protection -1 (token visibility), Signed-Releases -1, Security-Policy 4, Contributors 0. Honest constraint recorded: the aggregate is weighted and Code-Review/Contributors depend on sustained multi-party behavior — near-term reachable is ~9; a literal 10.0 requires review discipline on the last 30 changesets plus org-diverse contributors, both outside single-agent control.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Live Scorecard code-scanning reconciliation 2026-07-15: alerts #17-#23 map to existing children pm-003j (3 Token-Permissions instances), pm-0yi7 (Fuzzing), pm-2d7k (Security-Policy), pm-obq2 (Code-Review), and pm-rrt1 (CII-Best-Practices). No duplicate items created; these remain outside the active SDK implementation bundle.
